story.iWant: clear visual feedback on conversion progress for each file and overall batch status
story.soThat: I can monitor conversion progress, identify errors quickly, and know when downloads are ready

tasks.task: Implement Batch Conversion with Progress Updates (AC-1, AC-4)
Implement Error Handling with Specific Messages (AC-6)

criterion.title: Batch Progress Indicator
criterion.description: Overall batch progress displayed above file grid with real-time updates
criterion.verification: - Progress text format: "Converting 3 of 10..." (dynamic count) - Progress bar visual indicator (0-100% filled bar) - Completion message: "All 10 files converted successfully" (or "8 of 10 converted, 2 failed") - Progress indicator hidden when no conversions in progress - Real-time updates as files complete

criterion.title: Per-File Status Indicators
criterion.description: Each file card displays current conversion status with icon and color coding
criterion.verification: - Queued state: Clock icon ⏱️, Gray (#999), "Queued" text, default white background - Processing state: Spinner ⏳, Blue (#0073E6), "Converting..." text, light blue background (#F0F7FF), animated spinner - Complete state: Checkmark ✓, Green (#4CAF50), "Complete" text, light green background (#E8F5E9), download button visible - Error state: X icon ✕, Red (#D32F2F), "Error: specific message" text, light red background (#FFEBEE), error message displayed

criterion.title: Smooth State Transitions
criterion.description: Transitions between conversion states are smooth with no layout shifts
criterion.verification: - CSS transitions (0.3s ease) between states - Background color fades smoothly - Icon changes instantly (no animation delay) - Status text updates without flicker - No janky updates or layout shifts during state changes

criterion.title: Visual Feedback During Processing
criterion.description: Clear visual indication that conversion is in progress
criterion.verification: - Spinner animation during processing: Rotating hourglass (360° rotation, 1s duration, infinite loop) - Progress bar shows percentage (0-100%): Filled portion blue (#0073E6), unfilled light gray (#E0E0E0) - Processing state visually distinct with color change and animation

criterion.title: Completion State with Download Buttons
criterion.description: Download button appears when file conversion completes
criterion.verification: - Download button label: "Download format" (e.g., "Download XMP") - Button styled as primary action (blue background, white text) - Clicking triggers browser download - Downloaded filename: original-name_converted.ext - Download button persists after download (user can download again)

criterion.title: Error State with Specific Error Messages
criterion.description: Clear error messages displayed per file with retry option
criterion.verification: - Error message displayed per file (not global) - Common error messages: "Invalid file format", "Parsing failed: reason", "Conversion failed: reason", "Unsupported format" - Error message truncated if greater than 50 characters (full message on hover) - Error state persistent until user removes file or retries - Optional: "Retry Conversion" button shown for error state

criterion.title: Cancel In-Progress Batch Conversions
criterion.description: Users can cancel batch conversion while in progress
criterion.verification: - "Cancel All" button visible during batch processing - Clicking "Cancel All" stops all pending conversions: Files in "queued" state revert to queued, Files in "processing" state abort conversion (if possible), Completed files remain complete (downloads available) - Confirmation dialog: "Cancel all in-progress conversions?" (Yes/No) - After cancellation, batch progress indicator updates: "Conversion cancelled (3 of 10 complete)" - "Cancel All" button hidden when no conversions in progress

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Enhanced Web UI/UX
doc.snippet: UploadedFile typedef with status field ('queued', 'processing', 'complete', 'error'), BatchProgress typedef (total, completed, failed, queued), Format Badge Component with BEM naming, CSS Variables for format badge colors

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Enhanced Web UI/UX
doc.snippet: upload.js module responsible for batch upload, file card creation, progress tracking. updateFileStatus() function to update card UI based on conversion state. Batch conversion orchestration in app.js

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Enhanced Web UI/UX
doc.snippet: updateFileStatus(fileId, status, errorMessage) updates card data-status attribute and UI. createFileCard() generates HTML with status section. Batch conversion workflow: Queue files, Convert sequentially, Update progress, Show downloads

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Enhanced Web UI/UX
doc.snippet: Batch Conversion Workflow: User selects target format, Clicks "Convert All", For each file: Update to 'processing', Call WASM convertFile(), On success/error update status, Show download buttons or errors, Update batch progress text

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Enhanced Web UI/UX
doc.snippet: AC-4: Progress Indicators - Batch conversion shows overall progress, Per-file status indicators (queued, processing, complete, error), Smooth transitions, Visual feedback, Download buttons, Error messages, Cancel button

doc.title: Product Requirements Document - Path A Enhancements
doc.snippet: FR-3.4: Progress indicators for multi-file conversions - Overall batch progress displayed, Per-file status indicators (queued, processing, complete), Smooth state transitions, Visual feedback during processing, Download buttons on completion, Error state with specific messages, Cancellable batch operations

doc.title: Architecture - Recipe Path A Enhancements
doc.snippet: Architecture Components: web/js/upload.js (Batch file handling, progress tracking, updateFileStatus function), web/js/app.js (Batch conversion orchestration, batchConvert function). CSS state-based styling using data-status attribute. Sequential file processing for simplicity

doc.title: Architecture - Recipe Path A Enhancements
doc.snippet: CSS Variables for status colors (Blue=#0073E6 processing, Green=#4CAF50 success, Red=#D32F2F error, Gray=#999 queued). CSS transitions (0.3s ease) for smooth state changes. BEM naming convention for CSS classes (.file-card[data-status="processing"])

file.symbol: Landing page structure with upload section from Story 10-2
file.reason: Current HTML to be extended with batch progress component above file grid - contains upload section structure from Story 10-2
Will be modularized into main.css, components.css, layout.css - need current styling patterns for batch progress component and status indicators
Existing UploadManager class will be extended with updateFileStatus(), updateBatchProgress(), and downloadFile() methods for progress tracking
Will integrate batch conversion orchestration (startBatchConversion function) with existing UploadManager
Existing WASM conversion interface - will be called by batch conversion loop to convert individual files
Existing error messaging patterns - will be reused for conversion error mapping (WASM errors to user-friendly messages)
Current file upload implementation - references for status update patterns (showLoadingState, hideLoadingState, updateStatus)

package.name: vanilla-javascript
package.usage: No framework - modern JavaScript with modules for batch conversion, progress tracking

package.name: browser-file-api
package.usage: FileReader, Blob, URL.createObjectURL for file handling and downloads

package.name: css-grid
package.usage: Responsive grid layout for file cards (from Story 10-2)

package.name: css-flexbox
package.usage: File card internal layout (header, body, footer)

constraints.constraint: Vanilla JavaScript only - no React, Vue, Svelte, or other frameworks. Maintain existing web/ directory structure and patterns from Story 10-2
Batch conversion uses sequential file processing (not parallel) for simplicity and predictable progress updates. WASM conversions are fast (less than 100ms per file) so sequential is acceptable
Use data-status attribute on file cards for CSS state-based styling. Store file state in UploadManager.files Map structure (fileId to FileData object)
All conversion errors must be caught and mapped to user-friendly messages. Do not expose raw WASM error codes to users
Support Chrome 90+, Firefox 88+, Safari 14+, Edge 90+ - no IE11 support needed. Blob API and URL.createObjectURL are universally supported
Spinner animations use CSS animations (GPU-accelerated). Status icons include text for screen readers. Color is not sole indicator of state
Progress updates must not block UI (less than 16ms per update for 60fps). Use CSS transitions for smooth animations. Batch DOM updates if converting 100+ files
BEM naming convention for CSS classes (.batch-progress, .batch-progress__fill, .file-card[data-status="processing"]) - follow existing pattern from Story 10-2
Cancellation uses boolean flag (conversionAborted) checked between file conversions. Cannot abort WASM conversion mid-file (too fast)

interface.name: updateFileStatus Method
updateBatchProgress Method
downloadFile Method
startBatchConversion Function
Batch Progress HTML Structure
interface.signature: Converting 0 of 0...
interface.path: web/index.html (to be added above file grid)

interface.name: File Card Status Section
interface.signature: clock Queued

tests.standards: Epic 10 focuses on frontend UI/UX with manual testing emphasis. Testing standards: Browser compatibility testing (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge), Responsive design testing at defined breakpoints (320px, 768px, 1024px), Accessibility testing with keyboard navigation, Device testing on real devices (iPhone, Android, iPad), Visual regression testing for progress indicators and status transitions, Performance testing for batch conversion UI responsiveness
tests.locations: Manual testing documented in story completion notes. Browser compatibility: BrowserStack or manual testing. Responsive testing: Chrome DevTools device emulation + real devices. Accessibility testing: Keyboard navigation, screen reader support. Performance testing: Chrome DevTools Performance tab for 100+ file batches

test.idea: Manual: Upload 10 files, Start conversion, Verify progress text updates (0/10, 1/10, ..., 10/10)
Visual: Verify progress bar fills from 0% to 100% smoothly
Visual: Verify completion message: "All 10 files converted successfully"
Edge Case: All files fail, Verify message: "0 of 10 converted, 10 failed"
Edge Case: Mixed success/failure, Verify "7 of 10 converted, 3 failed"
Visual: Verify progress indicator hidden when no conversions in progress
Functional: Upload file, Verify initial status "Queued" with clock icon, gray color
Functional: Start conversion, Verify status changes to "Converting..." with spinner, blue color, light blue background
Functional: Complete conversion, Verify status changes to "Complete" with checkmark, green color, light green background
Functional: Conversion error, Verify status changes to "Error: message" with X icon, red color, light red background
Visual: Verify spinner animation rotates during processing
Visual: Verify download button appears only in "Complete" state
Visual: Verify smooth CSS transitions (0.3s ease) between states
Visual: Verify background color fades smoothly (no flicker)
Visual: Verify icon changes instantly (no animation delay)
Visual: Verify status text updates without flicker
Visual: Verify no layout shifts during state changes (card height remains constant)
Visual: Verify spinner rotates 360 degrees continuously during "processing" state
Visual: Verify spinner stops when conversion completes
Visual: Verify progress bar filled portion is blue (#0073E6)
Visual: Verify progress bar unfilled portion is light gray (#E0E0E0)
Visual: Verify processing state visually distinct (color change and animation)
Functional: Complete conversion, Verify download button appears with label "Download XMP"
Functional: Click download button, Verify file downloads to browser
Functional: Verify downloaded filename format: original-name_converted.ext
Functional: Open downloaded file, Verify valid XML/binary content
Functional: Click download button again, Verify file downloads again (button persists)
Visual: Verify download button styled as primary action (blue background, white text)
Functional: Upload corrupted .np3 file, Verify error message "Invalid file format"
Functional: Upload .txt renamed to .xmp, Verify error message "Parsing failed: Invalid XML"
Functional: WASM conversion error, Verify error message "Conversion failed: reason"
Visual: Verify error message displayed per file (not global error)
Visual: Verify error message truncated if greater than 50 characters
Interaction: Hover over truncated error, Verify full message shown in tooltip
Edge Case: Error state persists until user removes file or retries
Functional: Start batch (10 files), Click "Cancel All" after 3 files
Interaction: Verify confirmation dialog appears: "Cancel all in-progress conversions?"
Functional: Confirm cancellation, Verify remaining files stop processing
Visual: Verify message: "Conversion cancelled (3 of 10 complete)"
Visual: Verify "Cancel All" button hidden when no conversions in progress
Edge Case: Cancel before any files complete, Verify "Conversion cancelled (0 of 10 complete)"
Edge Case: Completed files remain complete after cancellation (downloads available)
Performance: Upload 100 files, Start conversion, Verify UI remains responsive during batch (no freezing)
Performance: Measure progress update frequency, Verify less than 16ms per update for 60fps
Edge Case: Upload 1 file, Verify singular "Converting 1 of 1..."
Edge Case: All files fail, Verify "0 of 10 converted, 10 failed"
Edge Case: Mixed success/failure, Verify "7 of 10 converted, 3 failed"
Edge Case: 0-byte file conversion fails, Verify clear error message
Browser Compatibility: Test progress indicators on Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge
Browser Compatibility: Test spinner animation on all browsers
Accessibility: Verify status icons include text for screen readers
Accessibility: Verify color is not sole indicator of state (icons + text used)
Integration: Upload files (Story 10-2), Convert batch (Story 10-3), Verify seamless flow
Integration: Verify UploadManager.files Map updated correctly during conversion
Integration: Verify WASM convertFile() called correctly for each file
Integration: Verify error messages mapped from WASM errors to user-friendly format
